body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/` **Triggered by:** commit 30f0d27fb (PR #58009) by @m-nash ## What Changed This commit merged the Azure.Identity credential configuration schema definitions (`credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, `browserCustomizationOptions`) into the Azure.Core `ConfigurationSchema.json`. This completes the migration of Azure.Identity types into Azure.Core that began in PR #57200 — the credential types themselves moved first, and now their configuration schema definitions follow. The merged schema enables IntelliSense and validation in `appsettings.json` for Azure credential configuration, just as the 1.52.0 release added schema support for `Retry` and `Diagnostics` options. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG `Features Added` is empty for 1.53.0-beta.1**: The credential schema merge (adding `credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, and `browserCustomizationOptions` schema definitions to `ConfigurationSchema.json`) is not listed under `Features Added` — only under `Other Changes`. This is a user-visible feature that enables credential `appsettings.json` IntelliSense. - **README has no mention of configuration schema or DI support**: The `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` does not mention the `ConfigurationSchema.json` NuGet-embedded schema (for `appsettings.json` IntelliSense), does not reference the `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` guide, and does not reflect that Azure.Identity credential types now live in Azure.Core. - **README Key concepts list is outdated**: The Key concepts bullet list mentions `TokenCredentials` abstractly but does not mention that Azure.Identity-originated credential types (`DefaultAzureCredential`, etc.) are now provided by Azure.Core itself, nor the configuration/DI integration (`AddAzureClient`, `WithAzureCredential`).
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### 1a. Update `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` Edit `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` — replace the current `1.53.0-beta.1` block: ```markdown ## 1.53.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added ### Breaking Changes ### Bugs Fixed ### Other Changes - `Azure.Identity` credential types are now included in `Azure.Core`, enabling SDK libraries to provide configuration-driven DI extensions (e.g., `AddAzureSecretClient`) without requiring a direct dependency on `Azure.Identity`. ``` With: ```markdown ## 1.53.0-beta.1 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Extended the JSON schema embedded in the NuGet package to include Azure.Identity credential configuration definitions (`credential`, `chainableCredential`, `tokenCachePersistenceOptions`, `authenticationRecord`, `browserCustomizationOptions`). This enables IntelliSense and validation for credential configuration in `appsettings.json` when using `AddAzureClient`/`WithAzureCredential` DI extensions. ### Breaking Changes ### Bugs Fixed ### Other Changes - `Azure.Identity` credential types are now included in `Azure.Core`, enabling SDK libraries to provide configuration-driven DI extensions (e.g., `AddAzureSecretClient`) without requiring a direct dependency on `Azure.Identity`. ``` #### 1b. Update `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` **Edit 1** — replace the Key concepts bullet list (lines 24–30): ```markdown - Configuring service clients, e.g. configuring retries, logging (`ClientOptions`). - Accessing HTTP response details (`Response`, `Response`). - Calling long-running operations (`Operation`). - Paging and asynchronous streams (`AsyncPageable`). - Exceptions for reporting errors from service requests in a consistent fashion. (`RequestFailedException`). - Customizing requests (`RequestContext`). - Abstractions for representing Azure SDK credentials. (`TokenCredentials`). ``` With: ```markdown - Configuring service clients, e.g. configuring retries, logging (`ClientOptions`). - Accessing HTTP response details (`Response`, `Response`). - Calling long-running operations (`Operation`). - Paging and asynchronous streams (`AsyncPageable`). - Exceptions for reporting errors from service requests in a consistent fashion. (`RequestFailedException`). - Customizing requests (`RequestContext`). - Abstractions for representing Azure SDK credentials (`TokenCredential`, `DefaultAzureCredential`, and other credential types are now available directly in `Azure.Core`). - Configuration-driven client setup and dependency injection via `AddAzureClient`, `WithAzureCredential`, and `appsettings.json` schema support. ``` **Edit 2** — insert a new sub-section after line 32 ("Below, you will find sections explaining these shared concepts in more detail.") and before `### Thread safety`: ```markdown ### Configuration and Dependency Injection Azure.Core provides extensions for configuring Azure SDK clients using `Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection` and `Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration`: - `AddAzureClient` / `AddKeyedAzureClient` — register an Azure client in the DI container with settings resolved from `appsettings.json` - `WithAzureCredential` — automatically resolve and attach Azure credential from configuration - JSON schema embedded in the NuGet package provides IntelliSense and validation for the `AzureClients` section in `appsettings.json`, including `Retry`, `Diagnostics`, and full credential configuration options For complete details and examples, see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) guide. ``` **Edit 3** — update the `Next steps` section to add a link to the DI guide. Replace: ```markdown ## Next steps Explore and install [available Azure SDK libraries](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/releases/latest/dotnet.html). ``` With: ```markdown ## Next steps Explore and install [available Azure SDK libraries](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/releases/latest/dotnet.html). For configuration-driven client setup with dependency injection (including credential resolution from `appsettings.json`), see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) guide. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required. The `ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md` doc already contains comprehensive inline examples. No `#region Snippet:` placeholders need to be added to the README for this update. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` currently has all required sections. The changes above add documentation for newly available features without removing any existing content. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core` 5. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Azure Core] Docs: Update README and CHANGELOG for credential schema merge and DI integration
